sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.146 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). A classifying space for a group$G$is a connected$CW$-complex with fundamental group$G$and all higher homotopy groups trivial. A group is of type$_n$if it has a classifying space with finite$n$-skeleton. For example, type$_1$is equivalent to finite generation, and type$_2$is equivalent to finite presentability. Type$_$means type$_n$for all$n$. For$n 3$, does every group of typ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
